name: Business Development Corp of America
description_short: South Carolina-based SBA lender offering 7(a), 504, and SSBCI loan programs to small businesses for 68+ years with $2.07B invested.
description_long:
Business Development Corporation (BDC) is a Certified Development Corporation that has operated as South Carolina's primary small business lending partner for over 68 years. The organization functions as an intermediary between banks and small business borrowers, facilitating access to capital through SBA-guaranteed loan programs rather than traditional commercial lending.

BDC offers three distinct loan programs: SBA 7(a) loans ($100K–$5M, variable rate, up to 25-year terms) for general business needs including acquisitions and equipment; SBA 504 loans ($100K–$5M, fixed rate, up to 90% financing) for real estate and equipment purchases; and SSBCI loan participations ($50K–$1M) providing credit enhancement and fast turnaround. The organization explicitly serves small businesses in South Carolina, with 504 programs extending to portions of Georgia and North Carolina.

BDC distinguishes itself through its deep institutional history, having funded 3,335 small business loans totaling over $2.065 billion. The organization emphasizes its collaborative partnerships with community banks across South Carolina and offers flexible underwriting options, including limited collateral requirements and the ability to finance closing costs on 7(a) loans. Their team includes specialized lending officers with significant loan portfolios ($130M+ and $266M+ respectively) demonstrating operational scale.

However, BDC operates exclusively through the SBA lending framework, limiting flexibility compared to conventional lenders. Geographic restrictions apply (primarily South Carolina), and the organization targets established small businesses rather than startups without significant equity or collateral. Processing timelines, while marketed as faster than traditional SBA loans, remain longer than conventional business lending due to SBA guarantee requirements.
pros: 68+ years of operational history and $2.07 billion invested in South Carolina businesses demonstrates stability and expertise
Multiple loan program options (7(a), 504, SSBCI) allowing borrowers to match financing structure to specific use cases
Up to 25-year amortization periods reduce monthly payment burden compared to shorter commercial terms
SBA 504 program offers up to 90% financing for real estate and equipment, reducing upfront capital requirements
7(a) loans allow financing of closing costs, reducing out-of-pocket expenses at origination
Limited collateral requirements on 7(a) loans provide flexibility for asset-light businesses
Fixed-rate options via 504 program eliminate interest rate risk over loan term
Current published rates (5.482%–5.725% for 25-year terms) competitive for SBA programs as of March 2026
cons: Geographically restricted to South Carolina (with 504 extending only to portions of GA/NC), excluding most U.S. markets
SBA lending process involves federal guarantee requirements that typically require 6-8 weeks or longer, slower than conventional business loans
Minimum loan amounts ($50K–$100K depending on program) exclude micro-businesses and very small startups
Subordinated position in SSBCI program means BDC takes secondary loss exposure, potentially stricter credit requirements
No mention of startup-specific programs; 68+ years of emphasis on established businesses suggests less flexibility for new ventures
best_for: Established small businesses in South Carolina seeking $100K–$5M for equipment, real estate, or business acquisition
Manufacturers and real estate-intensive businesses qualifying for 504 fixed-rate financing with high LTV requirements
Business owners with limited collateral seeking alternative credit enhancement through SSBCI participation programs
diagnosis: BDC is best suited for established small businesses operating in South Carolina with $100K–$5M capital needs, strong credit profiles, and ability to provide collateral or business equity. The primary caveat is geographic limitation (South Carolina-focused) and SBA processing timelines; this is not a solution for rapid funding or businesses outside the service area.
services: SBA 7(a) loan guaranty program with variable rates and flexible use-of-funds ($100K–$5M)
SBA 504 debenture program with fixed rates and up to 90% financing for real estate and equipment
SSBCI loan participation program providing subordinated credit enhancement ($50K–$1M)
Commercial lending team support and partnership with member banks
